Off Calendar Nov-13-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: The two following matters were called together: PES-02-282860 Estate of Patrick Pun and PES-04-286216 Estate of Vivien Poon.

Appearances: Caroline Hinshaw and Richard Bryan for and with Thomas Benson (Administrator of Vivien Poon's estate). John Chu for Gordon Poon (Individually and as Executor of Patrick Poon's estate). Bette Epstein for and with Gary Poon.

Counsel thanked the Court for its efforts to issued tentative rulings on a Court Holiday. Ms. Hinshaw and Ms. Epstein agreed to work amount themselves to sort out the numbers and distribution of the family plot. Mr. Chu stated that he does not agree to the concept and that new counsel will be substituted in for Gary. Discussions with regard to Patrick's property right, attorney's fees for Gary, Speedgain judgment and distribution of the judgment. Counsel to have discussion outside of court in the conference room and the Court will check on them at 12:45 p.m.

At 2:17 p.m., Ms. Hinshaw, Ms. Espstein and Gary Poon are present.

For Patrick Poon's Estate, the Court is not adopting its tentative rulings and set status hearing on December 11, 2012 on close of estate. Charles Jonas will be substituted in as counsel for Gordon Poon (Administrator). Ms. Hinshaw will notify Mr. Jonas of the status hearing date.

For Vivien Poon's Estate, Counsel agreed to proposed order will include Thomas Benson (Administrator) will be indemnified for any money pay to Gary. Gordon's only standing to appeal is money to pay Gary. Other terms that are included and not included were discussed. Petition for Final distribution and Petition for Reimbursement were taken under submission pending receipt of proposed orders.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Ella Yip. Bailiff: Deputy A. Payne.

Off Calendar Sep-11-2012 Continued To Oct-30-12 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Bette Epstein for and with Gary Poon. Richard Bryan and Caroline Hinshaw for and with Thomas Benson (Administrator). John Chu for Gordon Poon.

Counsel agreed that the Petition for Instructions can be taken off calendar. Mr. Bryan addressed and explained why their bankruptcy related fees were high. Ms. Hinshaw stated that there are no estate taxes, explained Mr. Rodriguez' efforts, stated why they chose to pay the gift tax, that calculation of statutory fees were based on what was inventoried and that Lim and Lim's fees were incurred in Singapore. The Parties agree that Lim and Lim's fees should not be paid by the Estate. Counsel addressed the issue of common fund fees, explained how Vivien's estate received funds and who benefitted from the judgment. Counsel also discussed the issue of assets going into Patrick's Estate and Gordon having access to those funds. Ms. Epstein will file an itemization of Gary's attorney fees by September 25, 2012. Replies are due by October 9, 2012. The Court will then circulate a final order shortly thereafter. Counsel are to brief the issue of whether distribution of the judgment/all assets should be made to Patricks estate. The Petition for Reimbursement of Expenses and the Accounting Petition were continued to October 30, 2012 at 10:30 a.m., in Dept. 204. With regard to the surcharge issue, Ms. Epstein asked for disapproval of Mr. Benson's extraordinary fees and presented oral argument to support her position. Mr. Bryan presented oral argument to support the Administrator's extraordinary fees. The Parties also spoke of the three burial plots that were purchased by Vivien and whether this issue can be resolved between the Family with the assistance of Counsel. Counsel are to brief the issue of whether distribution of the judgment/all assets should be made to Patrick's estate. Status on close of Patrick Poon's Estate (282860) was continued from September 25, 2012 to October 16, 2012 at 10:30 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Court will send out notice on Patrick's Estate that it is considering on it's own motion to remove Gordan based upon the arrest warrant and bankruptcy.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy C. Garcia.
